english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Hebrews

verses.latin_vulgate: Caritas fraternitatis maneat in vobis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ φιλαδελφία μενέτω
verses.king_james: Let brotherly love continue.

verses.latin_vulgate: et hospitalitatem nolite oblivisci: per hanc enim latuerunt quidam, angelis hospitio receptis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῆς φιλοξενίας μὴ ἐπιλανθάνεσθε διὰ ταύτης γὰρ ἔλαθόν τινες ξενίσαντες ἀγγέλους
verses.king_james: Be not forgetful to entertain strangers: for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mementote vinctorum, tamquam simul vincti: et laborantium, tamquam et ipsi in corpore morantes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μιμνῄσκεσθε τῶν δεσμίων ὡς συνδεδεμένοι τῶν κακουχουμένων ὡς καὶ αὐτοὶ ὄντες ἐν σώματι
verses.king_james: Remember them that are in bonds, as bound with them; *and* them which suffer adversity, as being yourselves also in the body.

verses.latin_vulgate: Honorabile connubium in omnibus, et thorus immaculatus. Fornicatores enim, et adulteros judicabit Deus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίμιος ὁ γάμος ἐν πᾶσιν καὶ ἡ κοίτη ἀμίαντος πόρνους δὲ καὶ μοιχοὺς κρινεῖ ὁ θεός
verses.king_james: Marriage *is* honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but whoremongers and adulterers God will judge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sint mores sine avaritia, contenti præsentibus: ipse enim dixit: Non te deseram, neque derelinquam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀφιλάργυρος ὁ τρόπος ἀρκούμενοι τοῖς παροῦσιν αὐτὸς γὰρ εἴρηκεν οὐ μή σε ἀνῶ οὐδ᾽ οὐ μή σε ἐγκαταλίπω
verses.king_james: *Let your* conversation *be* without covetousness; *and be* content with such things as ye have: for he hath said, I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: ita ut confidenter dicamus: Dominus mihi adjutor: non timebo quid faciat mihi homo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε θαῤῥοῦντας ἡμᾶς λέγειν κύριος ἐμοὶ βοηθός καὶ οὐ φοβηθήσομαι τί ποιήσει μοι ἄνθρωπος
verses.king_james: So that we may boldly say, The Lord *is* my helper, and I will not fear what man shall do unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mementote præpositorum vestrorum, qui vobis locuti sunt verbum Dei: quorum intuentes exitum conversationis, imitamini fidem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μνημονεύετε τῶν ἡγουμένων ὑμῶν οἵτινες ἐλάλησαν ὑμῖν τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ ὧν ἀναθεωροῦντες τὴν ἔκβασιν τῆς ἀναστροφῆς μιμεῖσθε τὴν πίστιν
verses.king_james: Remember them which have the rule over you, who have spoken unto you the word of God: whose faith follow, considering the end of *their* conversation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus Christus heri, et hodie: ipse et in sæcula.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰησοῦς χριστὸς χθὲς καὶ σήμερον ὁ αὐτός καὶ εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας
verses.king_james: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Doctrinis variis et peregrinis nolite abduci. Optimum est enim gratia stabilire cor, non escis: quæ non profuerunt ambulantibus in eis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διδαχαῖς ποικίλαις καὶ ξέναις μὴ περιφέρεσθε καλὸν γὰρ χάριτι βεβαιοῦσθαι τὴν καρδίαν οὐ βρώμασιν ἐν οἷς οὐκ ὠφελήθησαν οἱ περιπατήσαντες
verses.king_james: Be not carried about with divers and strange doctrines. For *it is* a good thing that the heart be established with grace; not with meats, which have not profited them that have been occupied therein.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habemus altare, de quo edere non habent potestatem, qui tabernaculo deserviunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔχομεν θυσιαστήριον ἐξ οὗ φαγεῖν οὐκ ἔχουσιν ἐξουσίαν οἱ τῇ σκηνῇ λατρεύοντες
verses.king_james: We have an altar, whereof they have no right to eat which serve the tabernacle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quorum enim animalium infertur sanguis pro peccato in Sancta per pontificem, horum corpora cremantur extra castra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὧν γὰρ εἰσφέρεται ζῴων τὸ αἷμα περὶ ἁμαρτίας εἰς τὰ ἅγια διὰ τοῦ ἀρχιερέως τούτων τὰ σώματα κατακαίεται ἔξω τῆς παρεμβολῆς
verses.king_james: For the bodies of those beasts, whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest for sin, are burned without the camp.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod et Jesus, ut sanctificaret per suum sanguinem populum, extra portam passus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ καὶ ἰησοῦς ἵνα ἁγιάσῃ διὰ τοῦ ἰδίου αἵματος τὸν λαόν ἔξω τῆς πύλης ἔπαθεν
verses.king_james: Wherefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people with his own blood, suffered without the gate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exeamus igitur ad eum extra castra, improperium ejus portantes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοίνυν ἐξερχώμεθα πρὸς αὐτὸν ἔξω τῆς παρεμβολῆς τὸν ὀνειδισμὸν αὐτοῦ φέροντες
verses.king_james: Let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp, bearing his reproach.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim habemus hic manentem civitatem, sed futuram inquirimus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ἔχομεν ὧδε μένουσαν πόλιν ἀλλὰ τὴν μέλλουσαν ἐπιζητοῦμεν
verses.king_james: For here have we no continuing city, but we seek one to come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per ipsum ergo offeramus hostiam laudis semper Deo, id est, fructum labiorum confitentium nomini ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δι᾽ αὐτοῦ οὖν ἀναφέρωμεν θυσίαν αἰνέσεως διαπαντός τῷ θεῷ τουτέστιν καρπὸν χειλέων ὁμολογούντων τῷ ὀνόματι αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: By him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of *our* lips giving thanks to his name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Beneficentiæ autem et communionis nolite oblivisci: talibus enim hostiis promeretur Deus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῆς δὲ εὐποιΐας καὶ κοινωνίας μὴ ἐπιλανθάνεσθε τοιαύταις γὰρ θυσίαις εὐαρεστεῖται ὁ θεός
verses.king_james: But to do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.

verses.latin_vulgate: Obedite præpositis vestris, et subjacete eis. Ipsi enim pervigilant quasi rationem pro animabus vestris reddituri, ut cum gaudio hoc faciant, et non gementes: hoc enim non expedit vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πείθεσθε τοῖς ἡγουμένοις ὑμῶν καὶ ὑπείκετε αὐτοὶ γὰρ ἀγρυπνοῦσιν ὑπὲρ τῶν ψυχῶν ὑμῶν ὡς λόγον ἀποδώσοντες ἵνα μετὰ χαρᾶς τοῦτο ποιῶσιν καὶ μὴ στενάζοντες ἀλυσιτελὲς γὰρ ὑμῖν τοῦτο
verses.king_james: Obey them that have the rule over you, and submit yourselves: for they watch for your souls, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that *is* unprofitable for you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Orate pro nobis: confidimus enim quia bonam conscientiam habemus in omnibus bene volentes conversari.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσεύχεσθε περὶ ἡμῶν πεποίθαμεν γὰρ ὅτι καλὴν συνείδησιν ἔχομεν ἐν πᾶσιν καλῶς θέλοντες ἀναστρέφεσθαι
verses.king_james: Pray for us: for we trust we have a good conscience, in all things willing to live honestly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amplius autem deprecor vos hoc facere, quo celerius restituar vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περισσοτέρως δὲ παρακαλῶ τοῦτο ποιῆσαι ἵνα τάχιον ἀποκατασταθῶ ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: But I beseech *you* the rather to do this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus autem pacis, qui eduxit de mortuis pastorem magnum ovium, in sanguine testamenti æterni, Dominum nostrum Jesum Christum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ θεὸς τῆς εἰρήνης ὁ ἀναγαγὼν ἐκ νεκρῶν τὸν ποιμένα τῶν προβάτων τὸν μέγαν ἐν αἵματι διαθήκης αἰωνίου τὸν κύριον ἡμῶν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: Now the God of peace, that brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great shepherd of the sheep, through the blood of the everlasting covenant,

verses.latin_vulgate: aptet vos in omni bono, ut faciatis ejus voluntatem: faciens in vobis quod placeat coram se per Jesum Christum: cui est gloria in sæcula sæculorum. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καταρτίσαι ὑμᾶς ἐν παντὶ ἔργῳ ἀγαθῷ εἰς τὸ ποιῆσαι τὸ θέλημα αὐτοῦ ποιῶν ἐν ὑμῖν τὸ εὐάρεστον ἐνώπιον αὐτοῦ διὰ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων ἀμήν
verses.king_james: Make you perfect in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is well pleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ; to whom *be* glory for ever and ever. Amen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rogo autem vos fratres, ut sufferatis verbum solatii. Etenim perpaucis scripsi vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρακαλῶ δὲ ὑμᾶς ἀδελφοί ἀνέχεσθε τοῦ λόγου τῆς παρακλήσεως καὶ γὰρ διὰ βραχέων ἐπέστειλα ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: And I beseech you, brethren, suffer the word of exhortation: for I have written a letter unto you in few words.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cognoscite fratrem nostrum Timotheum dimissum: cum quo (si celerius venerit) videbo vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γινώσκετε τὸν ἀδελφὸν τιμόθεον ἀπολελυμένον μεθ᾽ οὗ ἐὰν τάχιον ἔρχηται ὄψομαι ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: Know ye that *our* brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom, if he come shortly, I will see you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate omnes præpositos vestros, et omnes sanctos. Salutant vos de Italia fratres.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε πάντας τοὺς ἡγουμένους ὑμῶν καὶ πάντας τοὺς ἁγίους ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς οἱ ἀπὸ τῆς ἰταλίας
verses.king_james: Salute all them that have the rule over you, and all the saints. They of Italy salute you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratia cum omnibus vobis. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ χάρις μετὰ πάντων ὑμῶν ἀμήν
verses.king_james: Grace *be* with you all. Amen.
